mechanism_narrative: Persistent high level of the Federal Funds rate raises the opportunity cost of equity capital and compresses valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If over any 5-year rolling window, a rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day returns is positive or insignificantly negative (p > 0.10), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 0.2 percentage points signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp from its level 3 months ago
failure_condition: If in at least 60% of trigger events the forward 5-day return is positive, or average 5-day return after trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in both retail sales and housing starts reflects robust consumer and investment demand that boosts corporate revenues and signals economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST are above their respective 6-month simple moving averages
failure_condition: If the joint trigger yields a negative average 20-day return or the Sharpe ratio of triggered signals is less than 0.3 over a 3-year window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising industrial production signals overheating and margin compression from input cost pressures, which hurts earnings and forward stock returns. This relationship reverses in easing cycles.
failure_condition: If the negative slope of INDPRO on 60-day returns in the tightening regime is not statistically significant (p < 0.05) or if the slope is positive in the easing regime counter to expectation, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high readings of the Consumer Price Index indicate persistent inflation that compresses real earnings growth and forces the Fed to maintain a restrictive stance, weighing on equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year period the correlation between CPIAUCSL level and subsequent 20-day returns is non-negative or the average excess return in high inflation months is above zero, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales reflects a virtuous cycle of labor income and consumption that drives quick positive re-pricing of equities within a week.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS show month-over-month growth exceeding their respective 12-month average growth rates
failure_condition: If the joint signal’s average forward 5-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the success rate is below 60%, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals a slowdown in residential investment and weakening consumer confidence, which depresses equity prices over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If after trigger events the average 20-day return is non-negative or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over a 2-year window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy is in a late-cycle expansion, above-trend GDP growth signals overheating and unsustainable demand that precedes a slowdown, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE below 5% and FEDFUNDS above 3%)
failure_condition: If average 60-day returns after trigger are not negative or if the signal is not predictive in out-of-sample testing over a 5-year period, hypothesis is rejected.
